title: Harden Validator-B pipeline after code review
objective: Fix the Validator-B runner and related harness checks based on code review findings before live Tier 3 use.
scope: Move large Gemini prompt payload out of command-line arguments and into stdin.
Restrict Validator-B to read-only operation and remove workspace-write acceptance.
Add total embedded context budget handling.
Expand Validator-B independence guard against Validator-A/Codex artifacts.
Add schema text to Validator-B prompt and stronger output validation parity.
Add required field parity to Validator-A result validation.
Make Validator-B fixture auditing resilient instead of hardcoded to one task path.
Update docs, fixtures, report, and validation evidence.
success_criteria: run-validator-b no longer passes the full prompt as a CLI argv value.
run-validator-b rejects sandbox modes other than read-only and enables Gemini CLI sandboxing with the boolean --sandbox flag.
run-validator-b applies a total embedded context byte budget and marks omitted files.
run-validator-b rejects Validator-A/Codex references in refs and changed_files, not just selected phrases.
run-validator-b prompt includes the validator-result schema content or an equivalent schema contract.
run-validator-a validates github_commit and tier_reclassification_reason as required result fields.
validate-handoffs discovers Validator-B fixtures without a single task-specific hardcode.
node --check, Validator-B dry-run, audit:harness, and Validator-A validation pass.
constraints: Do not include secrets or environment variable values.
Do not perform live Gemini execution unless Gemini CLI is available and configured.
Keep existing Task artifacts append-only where applicable.
Preserve Validator-B independence from Validator-A results.
